panel.title: Join Failure Details
issue.transport.title: No game-info handshake
issue.transport.body: The connection failed before the host sent game-info data. Network reason: {0}.
issue.hostRejected.title: Host rejected the join
issue.hostRejected.body: The host explicitly rejected the join request: {0}.
issue.version.title: Game version mismatch
issue.version.body: The host and local game versions are different.
issue.modSet.title: Gameplay mod mismatch
issue.modSet.body: The host and local gameplay mod lists are not the same.
issue.modOrder.title: Gameplay mod order differs
issue.modOrder.body: Both sides have the same gameplay mods, but their load order is different.
issue.modOrder.body.deterministic: Both sides have the same gameplay mods, but their load order is different. ModelDb hash is based on deterministic final content on both sides, so order alone should not change it.
issue.modOrder.body.existing: Both sides have the same gameplay mods, but their load order is different. Deterministic final-content hashing is not active on at least one side, so load order can still affect diagnostics through the existing initialization path.
issue.modelDbHashMode.title: ModelDb hashing mode differs
issue.modelDbHashMode.body: One side reports stable ModelDb sorting, but the other side does not. The other side may have this setting disabled, may be missing the capability, or may be using an older RitsuLib version.
issue.modelDb.title: ModelDb hash mismatch
issue.modelDb.body: The gameplay model database is different between host and local.
issue.modelDb.body.deterministic: The gameplay model database is different between host and local even though both sides use deterministic ModelDb hashing. If mod lists match, check runtime model registration, direct ModelDb patches, or different local mod files.
issue.modelDb.body.existing: The gameplay model database is different between host and local. Deterministic final-content hashing is not active on at least one side, so also compare mod load order and any library, patch, or runtime registration differences.
issue.modelDb.body.modeMismatch: The gameplay model database is different between host and local, and only one side reports stable ModelDb sorting. Enable the same ModelDb deterministic sorting setting and use a RitsuLib version that supports reporting this mode on both sides.
issue.network.title: Network failure
issue.network.body: The join failed with network reason: {0}.
toast.reportCopied.title: Join diagnostics
toast.reportCopied.body: Join failure report copied to clipboard.
connection.NotInSaveGame: Joining player is not in the saved run
